cross_scene_arcs.title: Leadership and Team Formation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny decides to join the neighborhood group through baseball, forms a complete team by including Scotty, teaches empathy, coaches batting, and enforces rules, establishing himself as the leader.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Established leader of a nine-player baseball team

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship with Scotty
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny initiates contact with Scotty, includes him in the team, mentors him in batting, and reassures him during challenges, deepening their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Supportive relationship with Scotty through shared activities

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting the Beast
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benny enforces the boundary around the Beast's yard, faces a crisis when a signed ball is lost, is motivated to retrieve it, jumps the fence, escapes with the ball, and resolves the conflict by gaining new resources.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Fear of the Beast and a lost ball
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Successful retrieval of the ball and acquisition of more baseballs

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Outsider to Teammate
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scotty starts by following the sandlot group to make friends, faces initial rejection, but after Benny's invitation, joins the game and gains the group's support during a crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Isolated and following the group with the goal to make friends before summer.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Accepted by the group, who unite to help him retrieve a lost baseball.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Earning a Father's Approval
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scotty seeks his stepfather Bill's attention by asking to learn catch, faces indifference, steals a prized baseball, but eventually bonds with Bill through playing catch.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Self-doubtful and isolated, seeking fatherly connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Bond confirmed with Bill, who acts as a father figure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Stolen Baseball Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Scotty steals Bill's signed baseball, loses it in the Beast's yard, and with the group's help, negotiates a replacement from Mr. Mertle.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: In possession of the stolen ball, risking his stepfather's anger.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Conflict resolved with a replacement ball and a new agreement.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Squints' Pursuit of Wendy Peffercorn
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Squints develops a crush on Wendy Peffercorn, impulsively kisses her after faking drowning, faces humiliation and a pool ban but earns her lasting smile, eventually marrying her.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Smitten and distracted by Wendy's presence
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Married to Wendy Peffercorn and co-owner of the Five & Dime

cross_scene_arcs.title: Squints' Role in the Sandlot Gang
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Squints begins as the team statistician, participates in group pranks, faces corrections and mockery from Benny, but asserts authority as a storyteller and strategist, proposing plans for group challenges.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Team statistician with positive group dynamics
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Respected peer contributing strategic ideas to the gang
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Occasional conflicts with Benny over skills and knowledge persist but do not hinder his growing influence
